channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Movie theaters erupt in chaos as part of 'Minecraft' social media trend
item.description: Rowdy crowds of young people have been disrupting screenings of the newly released "A Minecraft Movie" around the country as they take part in the so-called "chicken jockey" trend.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Report: Drug lord linked to DEA agent’s death freed
item.description: One of the three leaders of the Guadalajara cartel linked to the torture-slaying of Drug Enforcement Administration Agent Enrique “Kiki” Camarena in 1985 has been released in Mexico, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump must face Central Park 5 defamation suit, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge declined to toss a defamation lawsuit against President Trump over his remarks last year about five Black and Hispanic men wrongly convicted of a 1989 rape, dubbed the Central Park Five. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: NYC mayor says family of Spanish tourists died in helicopter crash into Hudson River
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair Thursday and crashed upside-down into the Hudson River between Manhattan and the New Jersey waterfront, killing six people, including a family of Spanish tourists, in the latest high-profile aviation disaster in the U.S., officials said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Pressed for evidence against Mahmoud Khalil, government cites its power to deport people for beliefs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Facing a deadline from an immigration judge to turn over evidence for its attempted deportation of Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil, the federal government has instead submitted a brief memo, signed by Secretary of State Marco Rubio, citing the Trump administration’s autho
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Carolina woman wanted in murder investigation found in Netherlands
item.description: Dutch police have located a South Carolina woman, who is wanted in the murder of her ex-boyfriend and the father of her child, in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Annual White House Christmas ornament honors state dinner culinary diplomacy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The art of culinary diplomacy, practiced by presidents at White House state dinners for more than 150 years, is the theme of this year's official White House Christmas ornament.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Six people dead after helicopter crashes into the Hudson River in New York City
item.description: Six people have died in a helicopter crash in the Hudson River in New York City, an Associated Press source says.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYC mayor says family of Spanish tourists died in helicopter crash into Hudson River
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams says a family of Spanish tourists, including three children, died Thursday in a helicopter crash in the Hudson River that killed six people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Foster mom accused of exchanging child for exotic animal in bizarre trade
item.description: Brenda Deutsch is facing multiple felony charges after Missouri prosecutors say she allegedly tried to trade a foster child in her care for an "exotic animal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers say airplanes bumped each other on runway at Reagan National
item.description: Multiple lawmakers on Thursday said their airplane was bumped by another plane while waiting on the runway at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Accused Austin Metcalf killer won't face death penalty or life without parole: DA
item.description: The teen accused of stabbing track star Austin Metcalf to death will not face life behind bars or the death penalty, according to a Texas district attorney.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 7 international University of Chicago students' visas revoked under Trump admin
item.description: "I feel very strongly about this that this is unconstitutional," one woman told NewsNation affiliate WGN News.
item.dc:creator: Alonzo Small, NewsNation

item.title: Trump reverses Biden rule restricting water usage for showerheads and other home appliances
item.description: President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his "beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bill could change requirements for arresting students who make a threat of violence
item.description: A state law makes mass violence threats against schools a felony — regardless of intent or ability to carry them out.
item.dc:creator: Kori Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Budget airline Avelo to fly deportation flights for ICE from Arizona
item.description: Budget carrier Avelo Airlines signed an agreement to fly federal deportation flights from Arizona starting in May, according to the company, whose founder acknowledged the decision may be controversial.
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida brings charges against a man jailed on federal counts of trying to assassinate Trump
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — A man already jailed on federal charges of attempting to assassinate then-presidential candidate Donald Trump as he golfed last fall will face additional state terrorism and attempted murder charges, Florida's attorney general said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department moves to drop case against alleged top MS-13 leader, seeks to deport him instead
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has moved to drop a criminal case against the alleged East Coast leader of the MS-13 gang less two weeks after he was charged, saying they plan to deport him instead of prosecuting him in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Software giant fires anti-Israel worker for hate rant
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Education Department withdraws from plan to address discipline disparities for Native students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department has withdrawn from an agreement to address disparities in discipline for Native American students at a South Dakota school system, saying it was wrongly rooted in efforts to promote diversity, equity and inclusion.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and SARAH RAZA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Residents in this Florida neighborhood are under attack -- from hawks
item.description: A Florida woman has a unique problem outside of her Sarasota business. A pair of nesting hawks is attacking anyone who walks through the front yard. "Everybody, the minute you go in the walkway here, you get attacked," business owner Odalys Hayes said.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, KTLA 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Trump tariff drama
item.description: Financial markets soared on Wednesday after President Trump issued a 90-day pause in almost all the tariffs that had roiled the global economy during the past week.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, NewsNation

item.title: Boozy battle: Maryland and Delaware fight over 'Orange Crush' cocktail
item.description: Lawmakers in Maryland voted Monday to approve the orange crush as the Old Line state's official state cocktail.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Egg prices jump to record high despite Trump's predictions and bird flu slowing
item.description: The increase reported Thursday in the Consumer Price Index means consumers and businesses that rely on eggs should not anticipate immediate relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UTA announces 'significant' changes to several bus services including new routes
item.description: The Utah Transit Authority said significant changes are coming to its services in Davis, Salt Lake, and Utah County.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: This federal rule helped clear air over America's most beloved parks. Trump's EPA wants to kill it
item.description: During a hike in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park in 1995, Don Barger climbed Chilhowee Mountain hoping to gaze across the valley below. All he saw was a wall of gray haze. Today, he said, he can see some 50 miles (80 kilometers) across that same valley to the Cumberland Mountains.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND and MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jury awards $1.68 billion to women who accused writer and director James Toback of sexual abuse
item.description: The judgement marks one of the largest jury awards since the advent of the #MeToo movement, as well as in New York state history
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, KRON4

item.title: S&P 500 sinks 3.5% as Wall Street swings from euphoria back to worries about US-China trade war
item.description: The losses for U.S. stocks accelerated Thursday after the White House clarified that Chinese imports will be tariffed at 145%, not the 125% rate that Trump had written about in his posting on Truth Social Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ballerina Ksenia Karelina freed from Russian custody in prisoner exchange
item.description: Ksenia Karelina, a U.S.-Russian dual national who was detained last year on treason charges, was released from Russia in the latest prisoner exchange, according to the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US egg prices increase to record high, dashing hopes of cheap eggs by Easter
item.description: U.S. egg prices increased again last month to reach a new record-high of $6.23 per dozen despite President Donald Trump’s predictions, a drop in wholesale prices and no egg farms having bird flu outbreaks.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sweepstakes giant Publishers Clearing House files for bankruptcy
item.description: Publishers Clearing House, famous for its big checks and sweepstakes, files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as it shifts to digital advertising.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WGN 9

item.title: Jewish families channel Passover story after devastating losses in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: PASADENA, California (AP) — Aty Rotter lost her family home and her spiritual home in the ravenous fire that scorched a large swath of Los Angeles County earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Author Jillian Lauren, wife of Weezer bassist, arrested after being shot by police
item.description: Jillian Lauren, author and wife of Weezer bassist Scott Shriner, was shot and injured by Los Angeles police and arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after pointing a gun at officers from her front yard, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration will screen for antisemitic activity in granting immigration benefits
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's administration will immediately start considering “antisemitic activity” on social media and physical harassment of Jewish people as grounds for denying immigration benefits, according to a federal agency announcement on Wednesday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lottery player claims he was cheated out of $95 million jackpot win
item.description: A Texas lottery player filed a lawsuit in Travis County on Tuesday alleging his May 2023 Lotto Texas jackpot prize was severely diminished by $95 million because a group of lottery retailers and a London-based sports gambling company conspired to rig a lottery drawing less than a month before his wi
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Are dire wolves de-extinct? Genetically engineered pups spark controversy
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Seven GOP senators sign onto bill to check Trump's trade authority
item.description: Seven Republican senators have signed onto a bipartisan bill that would require Congress to approve President Trump’s steep tariffs on trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5
